qa_pairs.question: What is the main purpose of this troubleshooting guide?
qa_pairs.answer: This page describes how to troubleshoot some issues that come up frequently when using our cloud service to operate a virtual machine (VM, also known as an "instance").

qa_pairs.question: What kind of solutions does this guide provide for cloud service issues?
qa_pairs.answer: This guide includes solutions you can try yourself and advice about submitting a support ticket, including what information to include in the ticket.

qa_pairs.question: Can all issues with the cloud service be resolved by the user?
qa_pairs.answer: No, not all issues can be solved by you, the user; some things require a system administrator.

qa_pairs.question: What does it indicate if the guide suggests submitting a support ticket?
qa_pairs.answer: If you work through this guide and it advises you to submit a ticket, it is likely an issue which you cannot easily solve yourself.

qa_pairs.question: What is the initial requirement for logging in to the cloud service?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to specifically apply for a cloud project in order to log in to our cloud service.

qa_pairs.question: What error message will I receive if I haven't been granted a cloud project and try to log in?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have not applied for and been granted a cloud project, you will get the error message “Invalid Credentials”.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the application form for a cloud project?
qa_pairs.answer: You can apply for a cloud project using the CC cloud project and RAS request form found at https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeU_BoRk5cEz3AvVLf3e9yZJq-OvcFCQ-mg7p4AWXmUkd5rTw/viewform.

qa_pairs.question: How long does it typically take for a cloud project request to be approved?
qa_pairs.answer: Once you have applied for a cloud project, it can take a few days for your request to be approved.

qa_pairs.question: What information will I receive upon approval of my cloud project?
qa_pairs.answer: When your cloud project is approved, you will receive an email with important information for accessing your project.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I haven't received a confirmation email for my cloud project after more than 3 business days?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have not received this confirmation email, but more than 3 business days have passed since you submitted your request, submit a ticket to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca with your name, institution, and the email address you used to submit the request.

qa_pairs.question: How can I determine which cloud is hosting my project?
qa_pairs.answer: Your confirmation email will tell you which cloud is hosting your project.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find login links for different clouds?
qa_pairs.answer: Login links for the different clouds can be found on the Cloud Wiki page in the section “Using the Cloud”.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I have a confirmed cloud project but am still unable to log in?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have a confirmed cloud project and are unable to log in, check the System Status page to see if there is an incident affecting service on your cloud.

qa_pairs.question: Which username should I use when logging in to the cloud service?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to use your username, the same as you would use to log in to an HPC cluster, and not your email address.

qa_pairs.question: How can I test if my login issue is related to my username or password?
qa_pairs.answer: You can test logging in at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/security/login to see whether it is an issue with your username or password.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my password is rejected during login?
qa_pairs.answer: If your password is rejected, reset it by visiting https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/security/forgot.

qa_pairs.question: What information should be included in a support ticket if I still cannot log in to my cloud project after troubleshooting?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have followed these steps and still can’t log in to your cloud project, email cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca with your username, project name, which cloud you are trying to access, and a description of the steps you've taken so far.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first troubleshooting step if a virtual machine (VM) won't launch?
qa_pairs.answer: First, check to see if launching a VM would exceed some resource limit, also known as a "quota".

qa_pairs.question: What kinds of resource limits are typically placed on a cloud project for VMs?
qa_pairs.answer: Your cloud project has a limit on the number of VMs, CPUs, and GBs of RAM you can have in use at any given time.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I attempt to launch a VM that would exceed my project's resource limits?
qa_pairs.answer: If you try to launch a VM that would cause you to exceed any of these limits, the launch will fail.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check my current resource limits for my cloud project?
qa_pairs.answer: To check your limits, log in to your project cloud dashboard and on the left-side navigation menu click "Compute", then "Overview".

qa_pairs.question: What information is displayed under the "Compute" then "Overview" section of the cloud dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: It will show you how much of your allotted resources are currently in use.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I request more resources for my cloud project if needed?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need more resources for your project, request them using the form at https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeU_BoRk5cEz3AvVLf3e9yZJq-OvcFCQ-mg7p4AWXmUkd5rTw/viewform.

qa_pairs.question: What does the error message "Error: No valid host was found. There are not enough hosts available." when launching an instance imply?
qa_pairs.answer: The error message "Error: No valid host was found. There are not enough hosts available." suggests that there are not enough hosts available.

qa_pairs.question: What is a potential cause for the "not enough hosts" error when launching a VM?
qa_pairs.answer: You may have chosen an inappropriate Availability Zone when trying to launch the instance.

qa_pairs.question: What fields are included in the "Details" section when launching an instance?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Details" section, which is the first section you fill in when launching an instance, includes Instance Name, Description, and Availability Zone.

qa_pairs.question: What is the default setting for the Availability Zone when launching an instance?
qa_pairs.answer: The default setting is "Any Availability Zone", which allows OpenStack to choose a zone for you.

qa_pairs.question: How can I resolve the "not enough hosts" error if I manually selected an Availability Zone?
qa_pairs.answer: You can fix the "not enough hosts" error by setting the Availability Zone back to "Any Availability Zone" if you manually selected the zone yourself instead of using the default option.
